questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the name of a polygon with 10 sides?
questions.explanation: A decagon has 10 sides. Nonagon has 9, octagon has 8, hexagon has 6. Option A is correct.

explanations_tiered.definition: A decagon is a polygon with ten sides and ten vertices.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Polygon names are based on Greek prefixes: deca- = 10, nona- = 9, octa- = 8, hexa- = 6.
explanations_tiered.beginner: Decagon: 'deca' means ten, like decade (10 years).
explanations_tiered.intermediate: A decagon has 10 sides. Nonagon: 9, octagon: 8, hexagon: 6.
explanations_tiered.advanced: Standard naming: n=10 → decagon. Used in geometry and tiling.
reasoning_steps: Match prefix to number of sides.
misconceptions: Confusing decagon (10) with nonagon (9) or octagon (8).

publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Quiz on prefixes: tri, quad, pent, hex, hept, oct, non, dec.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.2 Sum of Exterior Angles of a Convex Polygons
questions.structural_anchor: Sum of the exterior angles of a polygon
questions.question: A convex polygon has 20 sides. What is the sum of its exterior angles?
questions.hint: The sum of exterior angles is independent of the number of sides.
questions.explanation: For any convex polygon, the sum of exterior angles (one per vertex) is always \(360^\circ\). Options A and D are incorrect because they depend on \(n\).

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.2 Sum of Exterior Angles of a Convex Polygons
source_trace.structural_element: Sum of the exterior angles of a polygon
source_trace.content_anchor: sum of the measure of exterior angles of a polygon is 360°

explanations_tiered.definition: The sum of the exterior angles of any convex polygon is \(360^\circ\).
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: This holds for all convex polygons, from triangles to 20-gons.
explanations_tiered.beginner: No matter how many sides, the exterior angles always add up to \(360^\circ\), one full turn.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Even with 20 sides, the sum remains \(360^\circ\) because each exterior angle = \(360/20=18^\circ\) and \(20 \times 18 = 360\).
worked_solution: Step 1: Recall property: sum of exterior angles constant.
misconceptions: Using interior sum formula for exterior.

flashcard.front: Sum of exterior angles of a 20-gon?

publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: find the measure of each interior or exterior angle of a regular polygon
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. The sum of exterior angles is always 360°, independent of side count.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The measure of each interior angle of a regular polygon is \(156^\circ\). How many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: \((n-2) \times 180 = 156n \Rightarrow 180n - 360 = 156n \Rightarrow 24n = 360 \Rightarrow n = 15\). Option A gives 144°, B gives 150°, C gives approximately 154.3°.

explanations_tiered.definition: Each interior angle of a regular \(n\)-gon = \(\frac{(n-2)180}{n}\).
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Set equal to 156°, solve for \(n\) algebraically.

publishing_metadata.flashcard_deck: Mathematics Unit 5 — Find Sides from Angle
publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Show both algebraic and exterior methods.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: find the measure of each interior or exterior angle of a regular polygon

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.2 Sum of Exterior Angles of a Convex Polygons
questions.structural_anchor: Sum of the exterior angles of a polygon
questions.question: A student claims that if each exterior angle of a regular polygon is \(18^\circ\), then the sum of the exterior angles is \(18n\). The student then solves \(18n = 360\) to find \(n=20\). Is the student's reasoning valid?

options.A: Yes, because the sum of exterior angles is always \(360^\circ\) and each is \(18^\circ\), so \(n=360/18\).
options.B: No, because the sum of exterior angles is \((n-2) \times 180^\circ\).
options.C: No, because each exterior angle is \(360^\circ / n\), not \(18^\circ\).
options.D: Yes, because all exterior angles are equal in a regular polygon.

questions.hint: The sum is constant 360°, so if each is given, divide to find n.
questions.explanation: The student's reasoning is valid: sum of exterior angles = 360°, each = 18°, so 18n = 360, n=20. Option B uses interior sum formula incorrectly.

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.2 Sum of Exterior Angles of a Convex Polygons
source_trace.structural_element: Sum of the exterior angles of a polygon

explanations_tiered.definition: The sum of exterior angles of any convex polygon is \(360^\circ\).
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: If each exterior angle = \(18^\circ\) in a regular polygon, then \(n \times 18 = 360\) gives \(n=20\). The student's method is correct.
explanations_tiered.beginner: The student set the sum (360) equal to 18 times n. That's right because all exterior angles are equal and sum to 360.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: For a regular polygon, exterior angles are equal. So sum = n × (each) = 360. Solving 18n = 360 gives n=20. Valid.
explanations_tiered.advanced: The reasoning correctly applies the constant sum property and the definition of regular polygon (equal exterior angles).
reasoning_steps: For regular polygon, all exterior angles equal.
misconceptions: Confusing exterior sum with interior sum.

publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Highlight the correct use of constant sum.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: find the measure of each interior or exterior angle of a regular polygon
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer A. The reasoning is valid because sum of exterior angles is always 360°, so 18n = 360, n=20.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.3 Measures of Each Interior Angle and Exterior Angle of a Regular Polygon
questions.question: A regular polygon has each exterior angle measuring \(40^\circ\). Find the number of sides and the measure of each interior angle.
questions.explanation: \(n = 360/40 = 9\). Interior = \(180 - 40 = 140^\circ\). Option B would require exterior 45°, C exterior 36°, D exterior 30°.

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.3 Measures of Each Interior Angle and Exterior Angle of a Regular Polygon
source_trace.content_anchor: measure of each exterior angle is 360°/n

explanations_tiered.beginner: Divide 360 by 40 to get 9 sides. Subtract 40 from 180 to get interior 140°.
reasoning_steps: Divide 360 by exterior to get n.
Subtract exterior from 180 to get interior.

publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: find the measure of each interior or exterior angle of a regular polygon

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.4 Properties of Regular Polygons: pentagon, hexagon, octagon and decagon
questions.subsection: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle
questions.structural_anchor: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle
questions.question: A regular octagon is inscribed in a circle of radius 10 cm. Which expression gives its perimeter?
questions.explanation: \(P = 2 \times 8 \times 10 \times \sin(22.5^\circ) = 160 \sin 22.5^\circ\) cm. Option B would be for side length times 8? Actually 80 sin22.5 is 8 × 10 sin22.5 — that's not perimeter.

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.4 Properties of Regular Polygons: pentagon, hexagon, octagon and decagon
source_trace.subsection_anchor: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle
source_trace.structural_element: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle

explanations_tiered.advanced: Chord length formula applied to n equal chords.

flashcard.front: Regular octagon, r=10 cm, perimeter expression?

publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: solve problems on area and perimeter of regular polygons

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.4 Properties of Regular Polygons: pentagon, hexagon, octagon and decagon
questions.question: What is the radius of the incircle of a regular polygon called?
questions.hint: The incircle is inside, touching all sides. Its radius is the distance from center to a side.
questions.explanation: The radius of the inscribed circle (incircle) is called the inradius, which equals the apothem for regular polygons. Option A (apothem) is the segment, but the term for the radius is inradius.

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.4 Properties of Regular Polygons: pentagon, hexagon, octagon and decagon
source_trace.content_anchor: radius of the incircle is the apothem

explanations_tiered.definition: The inradius is the radius of the incircle (circle inscribed in the polygon).
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: For regular polygons, the inradius equals the apothem.
explanations_tiered.beginner: The incircle touches each side at its midpoint; its radius is called the inradius.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Inradius = distance from center to side = apothem.
explanations_tiered.advanced: Inradius r_in = R cos(π/n) for circumradius R.
reasoning_steps: Radius from center to side = inradius.
misconceptions: Thinking inradius = apothem is the name; apothem is the segment, inradius is its length.

glossary_entry.definition: Radius of the incircle; equals apothem for regular polygons.

flashcard.front: What is the radius of the incircle called?

publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer C. The incircle radius is the inradius (equals apothem).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.1 Sum of Interior Angles of a Convex Polygons
questions.structural_anchor: Deriving sum of the interior angles of a polygon
questions.question: The sum of the interior angles of a convex polygon is \(1260^\circ\). How many diagonals can be drawn from one vertex?
questions.hint: Find n from sum: \((n-2) \times 180 = 1260\). Diagonals from one vertex = \(n-3\).
questions.explanation: \(n-2 = 1260/180 = 7\), so \(n=9\). Diagonals from one vertex = \(n-3 = 6\). Option C would be for n=10; A for n=8.

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.1 Sum of Interior Angles of a Convex Polygons
source_trace.structural_element: Deriving sum of the interior angles of a polygon
source_trace.content_anchor: n-2 triangles, n-3 diagonals from one vertex

explanations_tiered.definition: From one vertex of an n-gon, you can draw n-3 diagonals.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: First find n from interior sum: n = sum/180 + 2 = 1260/180+2 = 7+2=9. Then diagonals = 9-3=6.
explanations_tiered.beginner: Sum=1260 → n=9. From one vertex, skip yourself and neighbors, so 9-3=6 diagonals.
explanations_tiered.advanced: Number of triangles = n-2 = 7, so n=9. Diagonals from one vertex = n-3 = 6.
worked_solution: Step 2: Diagonals from one vertex = n-3 = 6.
misconceptions: Using n-2 instead of n-3 for diagonals.

flashcard.front: Interior sum=1260°, how many diagonals from one vertex?

publishing_metadata.flashcard_front: Sum=1260°, find diagonals from one vertex.
publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Reinforce n from sum then subtract 3.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: solve problems on area and perimeter of regular polygons
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. n=1260/180+2=9, diagonals from one vertex = n-3 = 6.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A regular polygon has an exterior angle of \(18^\circ\). Which statement is true?

options.A: It has 20 sides and each interior angle is \(162^\circ\).
options.B: It has 18 sides and each interior angle is \(162^\circ\).
options.C: It has 20 sides and each interior angle is \(18^\circ\).
options.D: It has 18 sides and each interior angle is \(18^\circ\).

questions.explanation: n = 360/18 = 20. Interior = 180 - 18 = 162°. Option B would be exterior 20°, C and D have wrong interior.

explanations_tiered.beginner: 360 divided by 18 equals 20 sides. Interior = 180 minus 18 equals 162°.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: For a regular polygon, n = 360/exterior = 20; interior = 180 - exterior = 162°.

publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: find the measure of each interior or exterior angle of a regular polygon

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 5.4 Properties of Regular Polygons: pentagon, hexagon, octagon and decagon
questions.subsection: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle
questions.structural_anchor: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle
questions.question: A regular hexagon is inscribed in a circle of radius 8 cm. What is its area?
questions.hint: Area of regular hexagon = \(\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} s^2\) with \(s=r\). Or \(\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} r^2\).
questions.explanation: \(s = r = 8\text{ cm}\). Area = \(\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 64 = 96\sqrt{3} \text{ cm}^2\). Option B would be for r=4; C for incorrect factor.

source_trace.section_anchor: 5.4 Properties of Regular Polygons: pentagon, hexagon, octagon and decagon
source_trace.subsection_anchor: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle
source_trace.structural_element: Perimeter, area and apothem of regular polygons inscribed in a circle

explanations_tiered.definition: Area of regular hexagon with side s is (3√3/2)s², and for inscribed hexagon s = r.
explanations_tiered.beginner: A hexagon in a circle has side = radius = 8. Area formula: (3√3/2) × (side)². Compute: 3√3/2 × 64 = 96√3.
reasoning_steps: Recall hexagon area formula in terms of r.

formula_analysis.name: Area of regular hexagon from circumradius
